Tutorial:
## Battle

The first aspect of the game that is taught in the tutorial is the battle system.
When going into battle, a blue area can be selected to adjust the lineup of shikigami that are going to fight. To change a shikigami drag one in the card line to the position of your choice. The yellow determines which shikigami can spectate, you can put up to 2 and its used for raising purposes.

All characters' action orders will be displayed on the action bar. Tap the skill you want to use to select it. Following that, select the target the skill is going to be released on. Skills can be used to attack, defend, heal, and so on depending on the shikigami or onmyouji.

The skills can cost onibi, they are the energy points required to release special skils. You start the battle with 3 and can have a maximum of 8. It replenishes every 5 of your turns.

The view is able to be changed by dragging the battlefield. Also, the speed of animations can be adjusted between 1x or 2x speed.

At the battle's conclusion, different screens will show depending on the outcome. If victory is achieved, the experience gain and drops will be displayed, while a loss nets nothing. Screens are progressed through via tapping.

## Summon

Most shikigami can be summoned. Onmyouji can enter into a contract with youkai, upon which they are called shikigami. There are several ways to summon shikigami. One of the most basic methods to summon is to draw charms on amulets. Shikigami can also be summoned by contract scroll in the book of shikigami once enough shards have been collected.

You start with Sanbi no Kitsune, Yuki Onna, and a random N shikigami. They are fixed and cannot be changed.
:To know more about summon click here. To check all available shikigami click here.

## Level
All shikigami can be leveled to become a six-star shikigami. They can also be awakened which boosts base stats, unlocks or strengthens skills.

A shikigami is summoned at level 1 and can go up to level 40. Shikigami usually ar
Talisman Pass:
Talisman Pass is a feature that replaces the Ibuki events and the Kimodameshi as the Daily Quests system. Every month the focused character and the associated rewards will change.

## Format

Quests are divided into three categories: daily, weekly and monthly.

daily.png|Daily quests
weekly.png|Weeky quests
monthly.png|Monthly quests

Daily quests are similar to what they used to be, with a few new additions - namely challenging Area Bosses and Demon Encounter, and doing wanted quests. Players can gain experience by doing these quests, which is applied to a 0-100 gauge every day. Players can open reward chests at certain experience milestones: 20-40-60-80-100. This works exactly the same way as the courage gain in Kimodameshi. Daily quests refresh at the daily reset, 00:00 server time.

In the Weekly quests tab, players are assigned 4 easy, 2 medium and 1 hard quests every week. These quests are random and can be refreshed using 10 jade each. Weekly quests refresh every Monday, at 00:00 server time.

The Monthly quests are also static, like dailies. Some, however, require server-wide participation and gives the rewards to all players upon completion, whether they took part in it or not. Monthly quests refresh after the Wednesday maintenance, four weeks after the start of the current iteration of Talisman Pass.

## Rewards

In the Rewards tab, players can unlock rewards at certain level milestones. All quests completed, in any category, adds to the experience count for the rewards tab. Players may purchase a Deluxe Talisman to unlock a better tier of rewards. The month's skin and loading screen illustrations are only available in the paid tier.

Another way to gain experience is by liking friends in the third and final tab - Rank. As the name implies, this tab shows a ranking of the player's friends. It is possible to gain experience only via the first four likes given in this interface. Likes give 2 experience normally, but very rarely they can give 10 as well.

Soul/Strengthening:
Soul can be strengthened to achieve better attributes.

## Strengthening
The "+" indicator on the Soul represents its level. Soul begin at level 0 and can be strengthened to level 15. Soul must be strengthened by sacrificing other Soul (where that Soul's experience is partially inherited) and they cannot level past the level cap, nor can they be ranked up to increase star rating. Strengthening also requires gold. The cost to strengthening depends on the stars of the Soul.

For secondary attribute chances when strengthening Soul to levels 3/6/9/12/15:
*If Soul has 4 secondary attributes, an existing secondary attribute will be randomly raised. Every attribute has 1/4 chance of being raised. Otherwise, existing substats have 9% chance, and new substat chance is 100%-9%*number of substats.
*If Soul's secondary attribute does not reach 4, then a sub-attribute is randomly added. Add chances are: attack-type 36%, defense-type 36%, utility-type 28%. (Includes attributes that already exist, it is possible to not get a new one)

## Levels
Soul require more experience to strengthen as their grade rises.

## Consumption
Soul at different grades and levels have different amount of gold requirements and experience yield. At level 0:
Normal=

|-| Aokichi Oni=

The other levels are too hard to find out as experience yield varies even with the same level - mitama could have their experience bars filled to varying degrees. What is known, at least, is that experience is not inherited in full.

Shikigami:
Shikigami are beings who have formed contracts with onmyouji to be summoned to their aid.

## Introduction
Shikigami are the beings summoned by the onmyouji to assist them. They form contracts which is recorded in a contract book. Shards of the contract book or a complete contract book (both the same rarity as the shikigami) for a shikigami may also be obtained to contract with the shikigami.

Once summoned, shikigami are found in the shikigami records.

In battle, shikigami are summoned through a medium like paper doll.

## Rarity
Shikigami have SP, SSR, SR, R, and N rarity. Rarity affects the number of shards needed to summon the shikigami and their rates in summoning.

N rarity technically has the distinctions of "materials" i.e. daruma, and "gekota."

## Attributes

## Base Attributes
Regardless of awakening:
Min Level=

|-|Max Level=

## Nurturing

## Leveling
:For exact numbers, see the Experience table

## Starring
For extra shikigami that must be consumed, 10K gold is required.

A shikigami must be at their level cap in order to be able to upstar. Of course this does not apply when completely maxed at level 40.

## Converting
Conversion consumes star conversion tickets, and this function is only available when selecting high-star units at 5 or 6-star.

The two shikigami switch levels and stars, experience if necessary, and nothing else.

## Skin
Some SR or SSR shikigami have the chance to be summoned with SP skin, which have several names. Ryomen's skin must be obtained through shop.

## Trivia
* It was possible to glitch the summon mechanism and obtain 1-star versions units
* R rarity was originally green

The Mall (商店街) is accessible from the Courtyard Scroll menu or Town. Here players can buy a variety of items such as illustrations, souls, skins, S-Jade, and packs among other things. To access the shops, players can tap on the icons on the map or select their icons on the bottom of the screen.

## Consignment Shop (寄售屋/委託販売屋)

The Consignment Shop allows players to trade S-Jade & special tickets for certain Skins, Skin Shards, Shards, & Accessories.

These tickets are Consignment Tickets & Jade Tickets. Consignment Tickets are used to purchase shards and accessories and maybe be purchased with S-Jade & Jade. Jade Tickets are received from doing Consignment Shop sales and can be used to exchange for Gold/Coins & Jade.

At the time only limited items are available for consignment, such as; collaboration shikigami shards, collaboration shikigami skin shards, and skin vouchers.

Only certain newer skins may offer these skin vouchers that can then be sold in the consignment shop 30 days after purchase.

To consign a shikigami shard, the player must already possess the shikigami and have not received the shards from "wishing" or "donation". These shards can then be sold and bought for Consignment Tickets.

Soul Zones/Fallen Sun:
Fallen Sun (日轮之陨/日輪の隕) is unlocked at level 37 and after beating the 10th stage of Orochi. 

## Introduction
Fallen Sun is a Soul dungeon that drops souls typically unavailable through challenging Orochi or Sougenbi, and is an alternative to buying them from the Mall. The three floors of Fallen Sun all require AP to access. This dungeon also has its own unique soul drop buff that can only be used 50 times from Monday to Friday, and another 50 times on Saturday and Sunday.

## Format
Fallen Sun can be challenged solo or co-op, and there are 5 shikigami and 1 onmyouji allowed in a lineup, exactly like doing Orochi and similar instances. Similarly, no spectator shikigami are allowed.

The 3 dungeons all contain the same youkai, however, the difficulty to defeat them increases, as their stats become higher.

## Special Mechanics
All push or boost to move bar effects will be ineffective.

All skills cost -2 onibi.

## Boss Skills

## Rewards
All Fallen Sun stages drop the same kind of rewards as Orochi, though the quantity and quality may differ. They will drop Hinoenma, Hyosube, Aonyobo, and Nuribotoke, as well as Seikichi Oni and orochi scales.

Soul Zones/Orochi:
:Not to be confused with the shikigami Orochi.

You can receive souls from defeating enemies at a location marked Soul (御魂) with three flags on the exploration map, south-west of the capital. Inside you can fight three-round fights, with the first two rounds having enemies determined by the stage, and the last round having Orochi and two more enemies. To unlock access to higher, more difficult stages that yield higher quality souls, previous stages must be cleared.
Stage 11 Orochi of the Maidens: Moans (巫女オロチ・悲鳴), requires players to have cleared stage 10 and cleared the 3 tutorials quests for stage 11.
Stage 12 Archnaga: Judgement (大蛇神·神罚), requires players to be level 40, to have cleared stage 11 and cleared the 3 tutorials quests for stage 12.
Stage 13 Aether Orochi (终焉大蛇·虚无), requires players to be level 45, to have cleared stage 12 and cleared the 3 tutorials quests for stage 13.

Clearing stages 10-13 can cause Ultimate Orochi (真オロチ) to appear.

## Drop Rates

List of features unlocked for players at varying levels.

## Format
The Orochi zone can be challenged solo, or co-op with a reduced AP cost, and there are 5 shikigami and 1 onmyouji allowed in a line-up. No spectator shikigami are allowed.

There are a total of 13 stages and the difficulty increases with each stage. 

## SPD Notes
* Stage 6: The 2 Hone Onna in round 3 have 130-135 SPD.
* Stage 7: Yama Usagi in round 2 has 135-140 SPD.
* Stage 8: Shouzu in round 2 has 142 SPD.
* Stage 9: Tanuki in round 2 has 147-148 SPD.
* Stage 10: Shouzu in round 2 has 164 SPD.
* Stage 11, Round 2: The boss Kuro Seimei has 156 SPD and Ootengu has 158 SPD
* Stage 11, Round 3: The boss Orochi in round 3 has 134 SPD.
* Stage 12: TBA
* Stage 13: TBA

## Rewards
Which type of Souls are available depends on the day of the week.

On weekends all the above and Azure Basan are available in stages 1-11, with the exception of the souls exclusive to stages 12. Additional drops for stage 10 include Orochi Scales and Reverse Orochi
Soul Edge:
## Trivia
* Since the damage calculation is dependent on the attacked shikigami, damage taken will depend on target's defense, higher defense on the equipper will not lower damage for either party
* It is triggered by attack, so the target does not need to take damage (ie; shielded) to be protected, conversely, it is possible for the equipper to take damage first due to different thicknesses of shields
* It's trigger causes both shikigami to be considered attacked, and shikigami like Mannendake will counter accordingly, it will also trigger passive of shikigami like Kuro Douji, depending on if damage is taken
* It can also be triggered by random target attacks due to them being considered as multiple single target attacks
* Shikigami equipped with it can still be targeted by random target attacks even if they do not appear to be in original spots, it's a display thing, not targeting
* In a formation with more than shikigami equipped with it, both will not protect the attacked shikigami at the same time
* The order that multiple Soul Edge steps in to protect is currently unknown, but is not dependent on health at all, so, its trigger chance is consistent at 50% no matter the number of equippers.
** Decision: Third shikigami is attacked > Soul Edge will protect them or not > one of the two equipped with Soul Edge will protect
** Obviously, if one shikigami with it is attacked, they may be protected by the other
* When equipper is under Shouzu link and target is not, it has a chance to trigger, if both are in Shouzu link, then it will not trigger
* As only damage is affected, the protected target may still be subject to various effects, such as triggering Senecio, Jizo, Scarlet, passives. And the attacker will still enjoy damage buff, Shadow, Seductress, etc.
* Teikon's previous 4 set effect was: 20% chance of inflicting silence on target when dealing damage

Demon Encounter:
Demon Encounter (逢魔之时/逢魔が時) is a mechanism to participate in cross-server boss challenges. It is unlocked at player level 15. Players can encounter "Solo Events" and "Shared Events".

## Introduction
At dusk, when the night swallows day and the human world meets the Underworld, when spirits and demons escape into the world above and unlucky humans may be dragged down into the Underworld, we begin the Demon Encounter. 

Demon Encounter is open from 17:00-23:00 (CN/TW), 18:00-23:00 (JP) and from 6:00-9:00/19:00-23:00 (GL). Players can engage in exploration and battles. There are a variety of Solo Events and a chance to encounter Shared Events such as Assembly Bosses. Exploration is done via the torii gate button. Each player has 4 exploration attempts per day. Great Exploration Rewards will be granted after all 4 attempts are used. On the first day of joining a Guild, Guild Medals will not be granted as exploration rewards.

No more new events can be found in the last hour of Demon Encounter, however, ones already found can still be challenged.

Demon Encounter closes at 9:00 (GL), 23:00 (CN/TW/GL) and 24:00 (JP).

## Solo Events

## Demon Seal
A shikigami appears and they can be challenged to give shikigami shards, like regular Demon Seals, but there is a wider variety of shikigami that can appear (including SSRs).

## Demon Quiz
:For more information, visit the Demon Quiz Answer List page.
A letter appears and has 3 multi-choice questions. Select the correct one to get the shown rewards.

## Demon Chest
A treasure chest appears and requires 50 Jades to unlock the treasure shown.

## Mystery Realms
A random player's realm defense must be challenged to receive the rewards. Only one attempt is allowed to challenge the realm. Rewards are also given regardless of a win or loss.

## Mystery Missions
Slips of paper appear and a mission must be done to receive the rewards. Missions can be any one of the following:

*Clear a Soul Zone 3 times.
*Clear an Explore Zone 1 time.

Realm Raid:
Kekkai Toppa is a pseudo-PVP mechanism. It is accessed from the Kekkai Toppa button in Exploration. Just like Arena, a Judgement Flag will increase the damage sustained periodically so that the kekkai toppa do not drag on for too long. A successful kekkai toppa will give rewards such as stamina, jade, gold, kekkai cards, and orders.

## Individual
Is the default interface. Requires no stamina to do and no cooldown, save a 5 minute wait between refreshes, however, a challenge ticket is needed. Gives rewards at 3, 6, and 9 victories, as indicated by the toppa log. When all 9 kekkai defenses are successfully penetrated the list will refresh for more onmyouji to challenge.

## Defense Log
:For more information visit Kekkai
Kekkai defense is the team that is set up to prevent others from succeeding at Kekkai Toppa of the player's kekkai.

Players can check the records of how their defense performed against players that challenged it, watch replays from the past two days, and share them. Buggy and lengthy ones will not be recorded, however.

## Guild
No tickets are required, but player must be a member of an Guild. Guild kekkai toppa must be opened by a leader or vice leader, who have to select a ryou from many available, on a map.

If a rou toppa fails, the opposing ryou's player's name will be announced in the ryou channel so that others can attempt to defeat it. And a announcement will also be sent should a player look for a team to defeat a kekkai together

## Ryou Information
The ryou that was selected for the toppa will be shown with its name and emblem.

## Progress
Progress of toppa as a percentage (# of kekkai defeated/total kekkai x 100%) will be shown, along with a marker at 90% for successful penetration.

## Rewards
Number of Guild orders to be gained from penetration will be shown.

## Cooldown
Shows attempts left before having to wait for cooldown. Individual attempts for each ryou is 6, and each attempt takes a 10 minute cooldown to restore. Timer shows ti
Realm:
Realm, also known as a kekkai (结界/結界), is a major mechanism that is part of a Guild. A realm can be created by selecting the current GPS location upon joining a guild.

## Realm Defense
A Realm Defense can be set up to oppose attackers during Realm Raid. A formation of 5 shikigami can be set as well as the onmyoji. A message can also be set for attackers to see.

The realm skin that a player buys will affect a Realm Defense when someone attempts to challenge it. Certain buffs purchased by the guild will also affect the units in a Realm Defense.

The onmyoji and shikigami in a Realm Defense are controlled by AI and there is no way to set their skills or behaviour.

## Nurturing Shikigami
Shikigami are available to be nurtured in 6 spots in a realm, and it is the only alternative to nurturing shikigami in the shikigami records or from bringing them along in battle. Each spot has separate distribution of rewards from realm card, etc., so there is no way to increase the share of EXP that each shikigami gets, regardless of how many are in a realm (the EXP rewards for other spots will go to waste). Rewards can only be obtained if there is at least 1 shikigami inside the realm.

The nicknames of shikigami can be seen in a realm.

## Entrusting Shikigami
In addition to the 6 spots, there are 2 other spots reserved: one for the player to show their shikigami which can be entrusted to a friend's realm for 6 hours, and enjoy its benefits during that time. The other for a friend to entrust their own shikigami in the player's realm. Neither side has the power to remove each others' or their own shikigami once it has been entrusted.

Again, because all spots are separate, entrusting a shikigami for a friend does not derive from the amount of rewards a realm card will give.

## Realm Card
Realm cards can be installed in realm to receive various rewards.

Cards of different star grades give differing amounts of EXP every hour (rewards are not calculated by hour but in seconds) as s
Guild:
A Guild is an organization formed by multiple onmyouji, much like a guild or faction. This feature is unlocked at level 6. Joining a guild opens up a variety of game mechanisms. Guilds are also the basis for setting a realm, one cannot have a realm of their own until they join a Guild.

## Introduction

## Creation
It costs 50 beads to create a Guild. The name of the Guild has to be 2~5 characters (no special symbols), and it cannot be the same as an existing Guild. There is also a 50 word limit to the greeting message, and the content cannot be inappropriate. The Guild is anchored to the real world position at time of creation, so GPS has to be turned on to find the location. The guild is immovable.

## Positions
A Guild has at most 1 leader and 5 vice leaders, and the rest are regular members. In total, the number of members in the Guild cannot exceed 100. The leader and vice leaders have the most authority when it comes to management.

## Leader
The leader has the right to set and remove the vice leaders, make 1 member recruitment call to the system and world chat daily, change the greeting message, create audio welcome messages, accept/reject and eject members, buy tools from the temple, open the Guild Realm Raid, and begin the Hunt. The leader can also voluntarily transfer their position to another member and become a regular member, or involuntarily through the dismissal process.

## Officer
Vice leaders have the partial rights of the leader. They can change the greeting message, create audio welcome messages, accept/reject and eject members, buy tools from the temple, open the Guild Realm Raid, and begin the Hunt.

## Dismissal
If the leader stays offline for over 7 days, Guild members can choose to dismiss the current leader and apply to be the new leader through the unlocked dismiss button. At the same time all vice leaders will get a notification regarding the dismissal. The following day the guild bureau will elect a new leader from the pool of applicants
Wanted Quests:
Bounty is a mechanism located in the courtyard, which is unlocked at player character level 12. It is accessed via the "Seal" button underneath the Sakura tree.

## Introduction
Bounty is a method for players to gain Shikigami shards (including SSR), gold, jades, as rewards from completing missions.

Regardless of the progress of completion, the Bounty refreshes daily at 5:00 and 18:00 sharp, with a counter showing the next time it refreshes. Each refresh replaces the old bounty targets with 3 new ones. Players have a limit of completing 5 missions between each refresh: 3 of their own, and 2 accepted from friends.

Mission progress can be seen without returning to the bounty interface as defeating a target spirit pops up a scroll showing the progress.

## Missions Types

## Common Quests
The default missions clearly state the target and chapter locations. They are completed solo. They are accompanied by flavour text.

## Special Quests
There is a small chance for special missions to be given among the 3 missions refreshed.

Note that missions that allow inviting friends are limited to ones that are currently online, and some also have level limitations. The invite friends pop-up will automatically filter them in these cases. A successful invitation shows up as a message in the friend chat as well as the friend's own wanted quests.
:For more information visit the Location List page.

## Types

|-| Mystery Spirit =
:For more information visit the Hint List page.
Can be identified via a lack of a name and a visual.

Location of the spirits are not given, and their identity must be deduced through descriptions and hints or via the wanted quest clues section in the ingame Help.

|-| Friend Co-op =
The mission has two progress bars, one for each player, and a button to add the second player.

The mission rewards are like normal, but a friend must be invited to complete it together. Should a friend not complete a co-op quest, there is the option to remove them after 2 hour
Wanted Quests/Incidents:
The level of the bounty is known from the colour of its stripes.

A youkai formed from a paper umbrella, sitting by the road, looking very lonely, eyes crying bloody tears, seemingly quite sad. I wanted to console him, but he suddenly headbutted me, and the tip of the umbrella scraped me.

Sigh...... old people like us have no desires, except to have a decent sleep. But recently there's a lantern-holding kid, shouting in the streets at night, even this kind of wish can't be fulfilled......

## Nurikabe
I was walking home as normal, but what's that, when was that big rock there? I turned left, it moved with me; I turned right, it also followed, and then "bam" it came down! Ahh, my leg!

What a girl without a filter she is, with such a small stature and a spiked rod on her shoulders, it doesn't fit her at all. She's just so mean, she told me off and I was so embarassed, sniff sniff sniff......

So, so scary, even lanterns can become youkai! Let me tell you, absolutely do not go to that alley. There's a Chouchin Obake, licking whoever it sees, you'll get smelly all over!~

A youkai with a head of beautiful hair, seems to have high standards for beauty. Holds a long kiseru, spreading a heady fragrance. His way of speech is just too feminine, I just want someone to go beat him up, nothing else meant.

I, I originally thought she was a normal person. But I found that she held, something, something like human bone! My god, why is she staring at me, save, save me!

_routine:
Recommended Daily Routine (Onmyoji Global):
1. Sign-in: Claim the daily sign-in reward (jade/amulets) from the calendar.
2. Free Summons: Use the daily free summon at the Summoning Circle and draw the free mystery/blue amulets from sign-in.
3. Daily Quests: Complete daily quests (fight battles, do summons, soul raids, etc.) for AP, coins, and contribution; turn in for the daily quest chest.
4. AP / Stamina usage: Spend AP efficiently. Early game push Exploration chapters for story rewards and to unlock features. Mid/late game, prioritize Soul Zones (farm souls for your team) and Evolution (Evo) Zones for shikigami evolution materials. Use Realm Raid (Demon's Den/Yokai Realm) on guardians for merit/medals to spend in the shop.
5. Realm Raid: Attack other players' realms for medals (Honor/Guardian shop currency), then collect realm-clearing rewards.
6. Demon Parade (Wanted Quests / Demon Encounter): Draw and complete the wanted quest board for jade and rewards; cooperate with friends.
7. Sealed Realms (bosses): Spend tickets/AP on Sealed Realm bosses for evolution and awakening materials.
8. Weekly: Participate in Duel/PvP for ranking rewards, Secret Zone, Hunt (Soul boss), guild (Onmyoji Guild) activities, and weekly events. Clear weekly quests for extra jade.
Priority for new players: follow Exploration story to unlock all features, level a balanced starter team (DPS, control, healer), feed duplicate shikigami and EXP fodder, and farm souls only once your damage is high enough to clear higher soul floors.
